results.possibleIdentity: Andrew D. Plivka was a physicist and technical collaborator active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. During this period, he was a physics major at the New York Institute of Technology (NYIT) and resided in Williston Park, New York. Plivka was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established to foster collaborations between artists, engineers, and scientists. He was recorded in E.A.T.'s technical registry as a volunteer with expertise in physics. Prior to his collegiate studies, he graduated from Herricks High School in New Hyde Park/Williston Park, New York, in 1966. In his later life, Plivka relocated to Florida, where he resided in the Miami and Fort Lauderdale areas. In Florida, he was involved in real estate investment and served as the registered agent for a business named Yes We Do Windows, Inc. He was also an active member of SAGE of South Florida, an organization for LGBT seniors.
